headline: Starbucks China Shift To Licensed Stores Raises Questions On Growth And Valuation
summary: Starbucks has closed a joint venture with Boyu Capital that transfers a 60% stake in its China retail operations. Roughly 8,000 company operated stores in China will shift to a licensed model under the new structure. The move is described as part of Starbucks' effort to expand store count in China and adjust its profitability profile in the market. For investors watching NasdaqGS:SBUX, this China joint venture arrives as the stock trades around $90.37, with a 7.6% return year to date and a...

headline: Starbucks brings back two viral drinks not seen in nearly a decade
summary: After more than five decades of evolving menus, Starbucks is leaning into a powerful growth driver: nostalgia. Following years of rotating offerings and discontinued items, the company is now responding more directly to customer demand by reintroducing beloved beverages. The shift reflects a ...

headline: Veritiv Named to Fast Company's 2026 List of the World's Most Innovative Companies
summary: Veritiv Operating Company, a leader in innovative and sustainable specialty packaging, facility, and print solutions, today announced it was ranked No. 6 in the Design category on Fast Company's 2026 World's Most Innovative Companies list, recognizing organizations that are redefining industries through creativity, impact, and innovation.
